title: campanula glomerata subsp. maleevii
intro: Campanula glomerata subsp. maleevii, also known as the Caucasian bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Campanula glomerata subsp. maleevii is often prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
